reasoning:
The BoE recently cut the Bank Rate to 3.75% on December 18, 2025, which abruptly dropped the SONIA to 3.7236 as of December 19. 
1. Dec 28, 2025: Rates tend to slightly mean-revert or spike slightly during year-end window dressing. Since 3.7236 appears slightly over-corrected (it was 3.7264 on Dec 18), we lean toward a high probability it bounces over 3.7236 by late December.
2. Jan 20, 2026: No new BoE rate decision before Feb 2026, meaning SONIA will hover around its ~3.725 mean. It usually features tiny upward drifts, sustaining a moderate probability of being slightly above 3.7236.
3. Mar 21, 2026: The forecast flips. Traders broadly price 3-4 BOE rate cuts in 2026. The next MPC meetings are in Feb and March. With a likely cut dropping SONIA into the 3.47% range, an increase relative to Dec 21 is unlikely.

As mandated, uncertainty strictly increases with horizon: |0.76 - 0.50| > |0.62 - 0.50| > |0.42 - 0.50|.
